Description: Caloptilia suberinella genome assembly, chromosome: 16
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Arthropoda; Hexapoda; Insecta; Pterygota; Neoptera; Endopterygota; Lepidoptera; Glossata; Ditrysia; Tineoidea; Gracillariidae; Caloptilia
Assembly: The assembly ilCalSube1.hap1.1 is based on 79x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes. Z chromosome identified based on BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements ( https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4).The Z and W chromosomes were identified based on its single-copy status within a diploid assembly.A haplotypic inversion was observed in the region on chromosome 1 (1,083 - 6,704 Kbp).Regions of sequence homology were observed between chromosome 2 and the W chromosome.The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 28 (6,992 - 8,112 Kbp) are unknown.Some unplaced repeat-rich scaffolds may represent B chromosomes, although this has not been verified.
